title: [v5.4] some CI backports + test fixes for rawhide
body:
Backports of
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25561
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25658
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25673 (Only one commit because the machine test does not exist here)

These should result in less flakes and faster windows machine testing so the next release should be able to get out a bit faster.
Also the ncat changes are needed to pass test in rawhide so we need them for gating as well.
 

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v5.4/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mheon PTAL, just to make the next release easier on us and also to have the ncat fixes for fedora gating which @lsm5 pinged me about.

Oh my,  checkpointing on f40 system to be completely busted.
Since we didn't test f40 on main due the go 1.23 requirement we never noticed. And nobody seem to have reported any issues about it either so far AFAIK.
I see two options disable f40 testing like on main (would also allow us to bump to go 1.23 to fix the crypto CVE) or just skip all checkpointing tests.

Also

concurrent map writes in c/storage during a pull? The sounds like a real bug. 

created https://github.com/containers/storage/issues/2297 for it

Repushed with f40 dropped from testing like done on main, that also open the door to bump to go 1.23 on this branch if wanted.

I didn't want to figure out how to add a ton of skips for all checkpoint failures, I have reported https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2355314 for it. (same bug as we saw on rawhide and f41 but they never backported the criu fixes)
@mheon Should be good to merge now
